faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Orange homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic buildup inside drains, pipes, and sewer lines. In Orange's older homes, aging plumbing systems can develop biofilm accumulation that provides ideal breeding conditions. Poor ventilation in bathrooms and basements, common in historic properties, can also contribute to moisture issues that attract these pests.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Orange, NJ?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Orange properties take 1-2 hours depending on the size of your home or business and the severity of the infestation. Our technicians will inspect all drains, apply bio-enzymatic treatments, and provide follow-up recommendations to prevent future issues.

faqs.question: Is the drain fly treatment safe for my Orange home?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products and eco-friendly bio-enzymatic cleaners that are safe for children, pets, and the environment. Our methods focus on breaking down organic matter in drains rather than harsh chemicals, making them ideal for Orange's residential neighborhoods and food service businesses.
